---
title: "Penguin Plots"
author: "Priyanka"
date: "2024-03-07"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Graph displaying something
```{r}
plot(cars, type="o", col="green")

```

# Some basic formatting options

in the next line, I will be starting a new paragraph. To do this, I have to end a line with two spaces.  
Here is the new paragraph.

I will be inserting italics in the next word: _beautiful_, using single underscores. Another way to write *beautiful* is to use asterisks: *beautiful*.  To apply bold, we use two asterisks: **bold is beautiful**

# Penguin Plots: Practice analysis
## Setting up my environment
Notes: Setting up my R environment by loading the `tidyverse` and `palmerpenguins` packages:
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)
```
